turns.user_message: They told me to just relax and stop overthinking everything, like it’s some simple switch I can flip. I tried to laugh it off, but the comment just sat heavy in my chest all afternoon. I keep replaying the moment in my head and wondering why I always seem to make simple moments so complicated. It feels like I’m constantly tripping over my own words and leaving everyone to clean up the mess. I just wish I could handle things without turning them into such a heavy ordeal.

I keep noticing how their tone shifts whenever I start talking, and it makes me want to shrink into myself completely. It’s been happening for months now, and I can’t shake the feeling that I’m the common denominator in every awkward silence. I try to keep my thoughts to myself, but they always spill out anyway, usually at the worst possible times. I end up apologizing constantly, even when nobody actually says anything is wrong. The weight of it just keeps building, and I hate how predictable my own reactions have become.

Every single time they sigh or glance away, I take it as proof that I’m fundamentally exhausting to be around. I’ve convinced myself that my presence is just a constant drain on everyone else’s energy, and I can’t stop punishing myself for it. I replay every conversation looking for the exact moment I ruined the mood, and the list just keeps growing longer. I feel completely trapped inside my own head, watching myself push people away while pretending everything is fine. It’s exhausting to carry this heavy guilt around, knowing I’m probably the reason the room feels so tense.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This has been going on for a while.
